markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire after the event occurs.
markets.rules_primary: If Carlos Cortes records 1+ home runs in A's vs Chicago WS professional baseball game originally scheduled for Jul 11, 2026 at 2:10 PM EDT, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Player Participation & Settlement Criteria
If Carlos Cortes is scratched or not included in the starting lineup, the market will resolve to the fair market price.
If Carlos Cortes starts the game but does not record a plate appearance, the market will resolve to the fair market price.
If Carlos Cortes is not in the starting lineup but later enters the game, the market will resolve to the fair market price, pinch hit at bats will not count towards the market.
If Carlos Cortes is in the starting lineup and records at least one plate appearance the market will settle based on home runs recorded.
markets.title: Carlos Cortes: 1+ home runs?

If a retirement occurs, all markets that can be unconditionally settled based on play already completed will resolve accordingly. markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire after a winner is declared.
markets.rules_primary: If Igor Marcondes wins the Gonzalo Bueno vs Igor Marcondes professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Bastad Qualification Round 1 by a set score of 2-1, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The following market refers to the Gonzalo Bueno vs Igor Marcondes professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Bastad Qualification Round 1. If the match does not occur (signaled by a ball being played) due to a player injury, walkover, forfeiture, or any other cancellation (all before the match starts), the market will resolve to a fair price in accordance with the rules. If this match is postponed or delayed, the market will remain open and close after the rescheduled match has finished (within two weeks). If a retirement occurs, all markets that can be unconditionally settled based on play already completed will resolve accordingly. Any markets that cannot be unconditionally settled will resolve to Fair Market Price, as determined at the sole discretion of the Exchange.
